hypothesis: Incorporating calibrated uncertainty estimates from physics-based feedback (e.g., Thermo-Calc) into the Direct Preference Optimization reward—by modeling the feedback as a distribution and using a risk-sensitive metric (e.g., a lower confidence bound) across temperatures—will improve the out-of-distribution robustness, validity, and design-space diversity of LM-generated BCC/B2 alloy candidates, reducing drift observed in architectures like OLMo.
